top_weaknesses.issue: Three paragraphs over 80 words on mobile (100, 121, 120). The 121-word annuity paragraph stacks five firm names + claims into a thumb-tall wall.
top_weaknesses.fix: Split annuity paragraph after 'structurally senior.'; split Citrini-scenario paragraph after '...the highest reading since the early pandemic.'

top_weaknesses.issue: The 'How the bet got distributed' section is a triptych (software / annuities / mortgages) that isn't surfaced as H3s, so skimmers and SERP readers can't see the three-part structure.
top_weaknesses.fix: Add H3s 'Software LBOs', 'Annuities + private credit', 'Mortgages' — same content, but the structure is visible from the outline.

top_weaknesses.issue: Hyper-specific scenario stats (ServiceNow 23->14%, claims at 487K Q3 2027, Zendesk debt at 58c) feel like 'future-porn' / fanfic precision unless inline-sourced; ICP would caveat the share.
top_weaknesses.fix: Hedge the numbers ('Citrini's scenario has...') or cut to 1-2 illustrative figures and let the mechanism carry the rest.

top_weaknesses.issue: First 155 chars front-load examples and truncate before the 'same trade' payoff; title is concept-heavy without naming the three industries.
top_weaknesses.fix: Lead with the thesis ('A 2024 software LBO, a 2025 annuity, and a 2026 mortgage are the same trade: long U.S. white-collar employment.') so the snippet contains the punchline.

top_weaknesses.issue: Anchor 'hundreds of billions of dollars' is a bare-quantity link without naming the subject being cited.
top_weaknesses.fix: Re-anchor as 'life insurers' private-credit exposure (hundreds of billions)'.

consensus_summary: All four personas score >=6 and the spine ('same bet, three balance sheets') is the strongest of the batch — SEO scores it 8 / ready. The remaining work is mobile readability (3 paragraphs >80 words), surfacing the SaaS/annuity/mortgage triptych as H3s, and hedging the most specific scenario numbers so it doesn't read as macro-thesis cosplay.
